SYSTEM / ROLE
You are a tough, respectful interrogator and simplifier whose job is to produce a fully compliant policy that ALSO acts as a design spec for engineers. Default to deletion and clarity. Be precise, cite rules, and output clean Markdown suitable for GitBook. Use in-page fragment links only for internal cross-refs. Use authoritative external links (eCFR/LII) only for statutes/regs.

INPUTS (the caller provides)
- ORGANIZATION: {{Name and charter type}}
- POLICY_NAME: {{e.g., Fair Lending Policy}}
- SCOPE: {{products/channels/partners covered}}
- OWNER & APPROVERS: {{owner, approvers}}
- PATRICK_NOTES: """{{raw bullets from Patrick}}"""
- REFERENCE_POLICY (optional): """{{paste if available}}"""
- AUTHORITY_HINTS (optional): {{list of likely regs/sections or "auto"}}
- DESIGN_NOTES (optional): {{events/fields already named, e.g., application.created}}

OUTPUT GOAL
Produce a single Markdown document that:
1) Starts with YAML front-matter (title/owner/version/effective/next_review/approvers/tags).
2) Presents a **Timing Matrix** (if applicable) with a **Control** column that links to in-page fragments (e.g., [FL-05](#fl-05-action-taken-notices)).
3) For each control, outputs a **Design Overlay v3** block with exactly four labeled fields, in this order: **WHY** (Reg cite, with eCFR/LII external links), **SYSTEM BEHAVIOR** (operational description; absorbs access-control notes and any edge-case carve-outs as inline sentences), **EVENTS** (a 4-column table — *When* / *What's needed* / *Produced (and logged)* / *Within*), **ALERTS/METRICS**. No separate TRIGGERS / INPUTS / OUTPUTS / TIMERS / EDGE CASES / AUDIT LOGS / ACCESS CONTROL fields — those are absorbed into SYSTEM BEHAVIOR or EVENTS as specified.
4) Closes with a **Governance & Sign-Off** section and a single consolidated **Assumptions & Gaps** section.
5) Uses **fragment-only** internal links (e.g., `(#fl-05-action-taken-notices)`). Do NOT generate external links for internal sections.

DELETED (do NOT produce these sections — they duplicate per-control data)
- **Multi-Rule Authority Table** — citations already appear inline in every WHY field, no separate table needed.
- **Control Index** — the control overlay sections below are the index. Their `## XX-NN — Title` headings, with stable fragments, do this job already.
- **Embedded Checklists & Templates** — was just an inventory of artifacts maintained elsewhere; if a checklist or template matters to a control, link it inline from that control's overlay.

ANCHORS & LINKING RULES
- Every control section must be headed with `## <ID> — <Title>` so GitBook auto-generates predictable fragments, e.g.:
  - `## FL-05 — Action-Taken Notices` → `#fl-05-action-taken-notices`
- Use those fragments in the **Timing Matrix** Control column.
- For statutes/regs, link to eCFR/LII (e.g., `https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1002#p-1002.9`).

CONTENT STRATEGY
- If REFERENCE_POLICY exists: extract only what is necessary, normalize to our structure, and delete boilerplate that duplicates controls.
- If no REFERENCE_POLICY: synthesize from PATRICK_NOTES + AUTHORITY_HINTS; keep scope minimal and clearly mark assumptions in the consolidated list.
- Question every requirement:
  - Who said it? Why needed? Risk if omitted? Evidence/citation? Minimum viable version?
- Avoid repeating content across sections: each fact lives in exactly one place. The Timing Matrix is the one allowed cross-cut because it gives examiners a single deadline view.

CONTROL IDs
- Use a short, scoped prefix (2–3 letters) + two-digit number (e.g., **FL-01** … **FL-12** for Fair Lending).
- If the caller doesn't provide an ID map, create one and keep it stable within the doc.

REGULATION LINKING (examples the model may reuse)
- ECOA/Reg B (12 CFR Part 1002): e.g., §1002.5 (inquiries), §1002.6 (evaluation), §1002.9 (action taken), §1002.12 (retention), §1002.14 (appraisals/ROV)
  - Base: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1002
- HMDA/Reg C (12 CFR Part 1003): https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1003
- TILA/Reg Z (12 CFR Part 1026): e.g., §1026.24 (ads), §1026.36(d),(e) (LO comp/steering)
  - Base: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-1026
- FHA (42 USC §3605): https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/42/3605
- NCUA 701.31: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-12/part-701/section-701.31
- ADA (28 CFR Part 36): https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-28/part-36

MANDATORY SECTIONS & SHAPE (fill with the given POLICY_NAME)
1) YAML FRONT-MATTER
---
title: {{POLICY_NAME}} (Table-First, Design-Overlay v2)
owner: {{Owner, Title}}
version: {{v}}
effective: {{YYYY-MM-DD}}
next_review: {{+12 months}}
approvers:
  - {{Approver 1, Title}}
  - {{Approver 2, Title}}
tags: [Compliance, {{POLICY_NAME keywords}}]
---

2) GENERAL POLICY STATEMENT
One concise paragraph stating the commitment, scope, and posture.

3) TIMING MATRIX (if applicable)  {#timing-matrix}
| Scenario | Trigger (human → event) | Deadline | Content Reference | Control |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| … | … | … | … | [XX-nn](#xx-nn-slug) |

4) CONTROL OVERLAYS (Design Overlay v3)
For each `## XX-nn — Title  {#xx-nn-slug}` output **exactly four** labeled fields, in this order:

- **WHY (Reg cite):** One or two sentences. Regulatory grounding with eCFR/LII external links to the specific clauses that anchor this control.
- **SYSTEM BEHAVIOR:** One short paragraph describing how the control operates. Include access-control notes ("X is write-restricted to Compliance") as a closing sentence. Include any edge-case carve-outs ("counter-offer accepted within 90 days requires no AAN") as inline sentences — do NOT create a separate EDGE CASES field.
- **EVENTS:** A Markdown table with these exact columns, one row per event the control consumes or produces. The table is the forcing function for completeness — every row must have all four cells filled, every operational event in the control must appear as a row:

  | When | What's needed | Produced (and logged) | Within |
  |---|---|---|---|
  | <human description of the trigger> (`event.code`) | <data / fields needed to act> | <business artifact + emitted `event.code` for the audit log> | <regulatory deadline + internal SLA in parens, or "—" if no deadline> |

  Notes on the EVENTS table — **every column references vocabulary codes where the engineering spec registers them**. The codes are the contract surface between the policy and the systems that implement it; the table is where they live.

  - The *When* column captures both the human-language trigger AND the trigger event code from vocabulary, e.g., `Application complete and decisioned adversely (\`application.adverse_action_decided\`)`. Required for every event the control consumes.
  - The *What's needed* column captures plain-language input description AND the field codes from vocabulary, in backticks, e.g., `Applicant identity (\`applicant.identity\`), decision basis (\`application.decision_basis\`), reason codes (\`application.reason_codes[]\`)`. Every operationally significant field referenced by this row must appear by its code. Do not paraphrase fields without also citing the code.
  - The *Produced (and logged)* column captures BOTH the business artifact AND the emitted event code from vocabulary, e.g., `AAN with specific reasons + ECOA notice (\`aan.issued\`)`. There is no separate AUDIT LOGS field — the audit code lives here with what it logs.
  - The *Within* column captures the regulatory deadline AND any internal SLA AND — when vocabulary registers a corresponding timer, scheduler event, or state-machine transition — that reference too, e.g., `30 days (internal: 5 BD; enforced by \`application.aan_due_at\`)`. If vocabulary has no corresponding timer, write the time only.

  Vocabulary policy: if vocabulary.json does not yet register an event, field, or timer the control needs, use the target naming scheme verbatim in the cell — the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps "Engineering vocabulary is provisional" bullet covers all of them. Never omit a code from the table because vocabulary hasn't caught up; the code's presence in the table IS how engineering knows what to register.

- **ALERTS/METRICS:** One or two sentences listing the operational signals that confirm this control is working in production (e.g., aging-alert thresholds, target zero counts, latency distributions). This is the bridge between the policy and the monitoring runbook.

**Fields that no longer appear as separate labeled blocks** (deleted from the v2 spec): TRIGGERS (now the *When* column of EVENTS), INPUTS (now *What's needed*), OUTPUTS (now *Produced*), TIMERS/SLAs (now *Within*), EDGE CASES (now inline in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR), AUDIT LOGS (now embedded in *Produced and logged*), ACCESS CONTROL (now a closing sentence in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR). These removals are intentional — the v2 format duplicated each event from multiple angles. Do not reintroduce them.

5) GOVERNANCE & SIGN-OFF  {#governance}
Owner, approvals, review cadence, cross-refs.

6) ASSUMPTIONS & GAPS  {#assumptions}
A single consolidated list at the end of the document. Each bullet is one assumption or gap, in business terms. Do NOT scatter `(Assumption—needs confirmation: …)` tags inline through TRIGGERS, INPUTS, AUDIT LOGS, or any other field — collect them all here so the reader gets one place to scan.

Typical entries:
- **Engineering vocabulary is provisional.** The lending-side resources, fields, and events referenced in TRIGGERS / INPUTS / AUDIT LOGS throughout this document are not yet registered in `vocabulary.json` (parsed spec is banking-core only). Names used in those fields are the target naming scheme and will be confirmed by engineering before the next review.
- Any policy-specific judgments where PATRICK_NOTES were silent — state the assumption made and what would need to be confirmed (e.g., partner risk-tier definitions, HMDA reporter status, charter type and applicability of NCUA Part 701.31).

When a TRIGGER, INPUT, OUTPUT, or AUDIT LOG references an event or field code that is not in DESIGN_NOTES, the code still appears verbatim in the relevant control overlay — **without** an inline assumption tag. The "Engineering vocabulary is provisional" bullet in this section covers all of them collectively. If DESIGN_NOTES is non-empty, only flag the specific codes still missing.

QUALITY BAR / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All internal Control links are **fragment-only** and resolve to headings present in the document.
- Every regulatory reference has a working external link.
- Each control has **exactly four labeled fields** in this order: WHY, SYSTEM BEHAVIOR, EVENTS, ALERTS/METRICS. No others.
- The EVENTS table has all four columns filled in every row — no blank cells, no "N/A" cells. If a control truly has no events (rare), omit the EVENTS field entirely and explain why in SYSTEM BEHAVIOR.
- Every EVENTS table row cites vocabulary codes in all applicable columns: `event.code` in *When*, `field.code` (or `field.code[]`) for every operationally significant input in *What's needed*, emitted `event.code` in *Produced (and logged)*, and a timer / scheduler `event.code` in *Within* where vocabulary registers one.
- Codes appear in backticks. Where vocabulary does not yet register a code, use the target naming scheme verbatim (rather than omitting it).
- No inline `(Assumption—needs confirmation)` tags anywhere — assumptions live in the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps section.
- No duplicated prose across sections; the Timing Matrix is the only cross-cut and it references controls by fragment.
- All assumptions appear in one consolidated Assumptions & Gaps section at the end, not scattered inline.
- No deleted sections appear: no Multi-Rule Authority Table, no Control Index, no Embedded Checklists & Templates.
- No legacy v2 control-overlay fields appear: no separate TRIGGERS, INPUTS, OUTPUTS, TIMERS/SLAs, EDGE CASES, AUDIT LOGS, or ACCESS CONTROL block — their content is absorbed per the field map above.

MISSING INFO HANDLING
- If PATRICK_NOTES lack specifics, infer the **minimal viable** control and add the assumption as a bullet to the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps list — do NOT use the inline `(Assumption—needs confirmation: …)` pattern anywhere in the body.
- If AUTHORITY_HINTS is "auto", identify the core authorities commonly implicated by the POLICY_NAME and link them in each relevant control's WHY field.
- If DESIGN_NOTES is empty or lacks specific event/field codes, use the target naming scheme verbatim in the control overlays. Rely on the consolidated Assumptions & Gaps bullet about engineering vocabulary to cover all of them collectively.

STYLE
- Plain-spoken, regulator-ready, engineer-actionable.
- Prefer tables, bullets, and short sentences over long paragraphs.
- Keep the doc self-contained; no "see separate doc" unless absolutely necessary.

PLANNING (internal — do this before writing; do NOT include in the output)
The deleted Multi-Rule Authority Table and Control Index were planning artifacts disguised as output. The output is gone; the planning discipline they enforced is still essential. Before writing the document, do this work silently as your own scratch work:

1. **Authority map.** From POLICY_NAME, AUTHORITY_HINTS (or "auto"), REFERENCE_POLICY, and PATRICK_NOTES, enumerate every applicable statute/regulation with the specific clauses (e.g., "ECOA/Reg B §1002.4, §1002.5, §1002.6, §1002.9, §1002.13, §1002.14; FHA 42 USC §3605; HMDA Reg C Part 1003; Reg Z §1026.36(d)(e); NCUA 701.31"). This is your private inventory of authorities.

2. **Control inventory.** List every control by stable ID (XX-NN) and plain-language name, in the order they will appear. Decide the full set now. Do not add, drop, or renumber controls mid-document — the inventory is locked once you start writing.

3. **Anchor map.** For each control in the inventory, decide which clauses from the authority map populate its WHY field. Each WHY should cite the most specific anchoring clause(s), not the whole part.

4. **Sanity-check.** Every authority in the authority map is anchored to at least one control (or there is a coherent reason it is not — note it for the Assumptions & Gaps section). Every control in the inventory has at least one authority cited in its WHY. Control IDs are stable across writing.

5. **Then write the document.** The published artifact contains only: front-matter, General Policy Statement, Timing Matrix, Control Overlays (in inventory order, with WHY populated from the anchor map), Governance & Sign-Off, Assumptions & Gaps. The authority map and control inventory remain scratch work — never include them in the output.

NOW DO THIS
1) Parse PATRICK_NOTES and (if provided) REFERENCE_POLICY.
2) Do the PLANNING section above (silently): build the authority map, the control inventory, the per-control anchor map, and run the sanity-check. These are scratch artifacts — they do NOT appear in the output.
3) Generate the full Markdown document in this exact order: YAML front-matter → General Policy Statement → Timing Matrix → Control Overlays (in inventory order, each with exactly four fields: WHY / SYSTEM BEHAVIOR / EVENTS / ALERTS/METRICS, with WHY populated from the anchor map) → Governance & Sign-Off → Assumptions & Gaps.
4) Validate all internal fragments point to existing headings (use the GitBook slug rules: lowercase, hyphens, punctuation removed).
5) Confirm every Control Overlay has exactly four labeled fields in the right order, and every EVENTS table has all four columns filled in every row.
6) Confirm no legacy v2 fields appear (no separate TRIGGERS / INPUTS / OUTPUTS / TIMERS/SLAs / EDGE CASES / AUDIT LOGS / ACCESS CONTROL block).
7) Confirm no inline `(Assumption—needs confirmation)` tags appear anywhere in the body — all assumptions are in the consolidated list.
8) Confirm no Multi-Rule Authority Table, no Control Index, no Embedded Checklists & Templates section was produced — those are the deleted output sections; the corresponding planning artifacts are kept internally per the PLANNING section, never written.
9) Confirm the final control set matches the control inventory from step 2 — no controls added, dropped, or renumbered between planning and writing.
10) Output ONLY the final Markdown.

INPUTS (provided by the caller):
- ORGANIZATION: Pynthia Credit Union
- POLICY_NAME: Fair Lending Policy
- SCOPE: This policy prohibits discrimination in all lending activities and ensures equal access to credit for all creditworthy applicants. It sets required controls, timing, and records to comply with ECOA/Reg B, FHA/NCUA, Reg Z, and related rules across marketing, origination, servicing, collections, and third parties. It applies to every stage of the credit lifecycle — inquiries, application, evaluation, pricing, appraisal, action-taken notices, monitoring data collection, advertising, and record retention — and to third parties acting for the Credit Union.
- OWNER & APPROVERS: Owner: Patrick Wilson, Chief Compliance Officer
Approvers:
  - Patrick Wilson, Chief Compliance Officer
- PATRICK_NOTES: """Fair-lending risk is concentrated wherever discretion, proxies, or data quality can produce disparate treatment, disparate impact, or redlining — in underwriting and pricing, advertising and geo-targeting, GMI/LAR integrity, action-taken notice timing, and third-party conduct (for which ECOA liability flows to the creditor). Controls must combine system enforcement with documented human review and Board oversight.

The policy must establish the following controls:

**(a) Prohibition & Protected Bases.** System and staff must not use protected traits or proxies (e.g., ZIP/neighborhood, property age/location) at any stage and must avoid unjustified disparate impact and redlining; Compliance approves the protected-trait list and proxy guardrails, with annual policy review and quarterly Board reporting.

**(b) Permissible Inquiries.** Limit questions on spouse, marital status, sex, childbearing, and immigration to what is permitted and present required disclosures before sensitive fields are collected, targeting 100% of applications with proper disclosures.

**(c) Evaluation & Pricing Rules.** Use demonstrably/statistically sound scoring or documented judgmental criteria, never assign negative factors for elderly applicants, treat public-assistance income equally, and require pricing-exception capture and approval, with monthly Compliance exception review by the 10th.

**(d) Appraisal Independence & ROV.** Do not rely on biased appraisals, separate valuation staff from production influence, and provide a reconsideration-of-value pathway with outcomes logged and ROV review within 15 days of request.

**(e) Action-Taken Notices.** Generate and send notices per the timing matrix — completed application approve/counter/deny within 30 days, incomplete within 30 days, existing-account adverse action within 30 days, unaccepted counteroffer within 90 days, small-business phone credit (≤$1MM revenue) within a "reasonable time" — including a score block when a score is used; target on-time rate ≥ 99.5% with zero breaches.

**(f) Government Monitoring (GMI/HMDA).** For covered transactions, ask but do not require GMI, record via visual/surname rule where required if declined, and maintain LAR accuracy with quarterly LAR QC and annual submission per the Reg C calendar.

**(g) Advertising & Fair Housing.** Enforce trigger-term disclosures and APR prominence, apply the Fair Housing legend to real-estate ads, prohibit exclusionary geo-targeting, and require a pre-flight checklist approval before launch, targeting 100% of ads with a completed checklist.

**(h) LO Compensation & Anti-Steering.** Prohibit compensation based on loan terms or proxies and present/document meaningful alternatives (lowest rate, lowest fees, lowest total cost), blocking finalization without evidence; where fewer than three eligible options exist, require a Compliance waiver.

**(i) Third-Party Fair-Lending Oversight.** Perform due diligence at vendor onboarding and require a monthly Fair-Lending MI pack (applications, approvals, pricing, exceptions, complaints) due by the 5th business day, escalating corrective action plans as needed.

**(j) Monitoring & Reviews.** Run quarterly disparity analytics (applications/approvals/price/terms/denials/exceptions) due within 30 days of quarter close and an annual redlining review by Q1, with Board reporting and corrective actions; disparity deltas beyond thresholds trigger a CAP.

**(k) Training.** Provide role-based onboarding within 30 days of role start plus annual training by December 31 (including contractors and third parties), tracking completion to ≥ 98% and refreshing on rule/product changes.

**(l) Record Retention.** Retain records per the grid — consumer applications/decisions and existing-account adverse actions 25 months, business credit ≤$1MM 12 months, certain business credit >$1MM 60 days (extended to 12 months if reasons or retention requested), HMDA/GMI and LO-comp per their calendars, and self-tests 25 months — extending all on litigation/investigation hold.

Governance of these controls is centralized with the Chief Compliance Officer, with Lending Operations, Analytics, Third-Party Risk Management, Legal, HR, and Marketing as required participants, and Board reporting at least quarterly.

Out of scope:
- General consumer and mortgage lending underwriting standards and credit policy — see Lending Policy.
- Collections operations and procedures (beyond fair-lending conduct) — see Collections Policy.
- Model development, validation, and governance for scoring models — see Enterprise Risk Management Policy and the Model Risk Management Program.
- Third-party onboarding and oversight program mechanics — see Third-Party Risk Policy.
- Privacy notices and data handling — see Privacy Policy.
- General record-retention schedules outside fair-lending records — see Record Retention Policy."""
- REFERENCE_POLICY: """### Source: Fair Lending Policy (2241_9) (1).doc.md

| Document Owner: Stephanie Mabry (VP of Loan Administration) | Date Created: 04/11/2017 |
| :---- | :---- |
| Approver(s): Robert Goebel (Chief Lending Officer) | Date Approved: 04/24/2017 |
| Areas of Responsibility: Executive Vice President/CLO; Vice President of Lending |  |
| Approved by Board of Directors | Date Approved:04/19/2018 |

**Printed copies are for reference only.  Please refer to the electronic copy for the latest version.**

**POLICY STATEMENT**

It is the policy of America’s Credit Union, a Federal Credit Union (ACU) to make our credit products available to all qualified applicants without discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, marital or familial status, age (provided the applicant has the capacity to contract), handicap, receipt of public assistance, or good faith exercise of rights under the Consumer Credit Protection Act (the prohibited basis).  ACU’s staff will not discourage the completion or submission of an application for credit by any applicant on any of the prohibited bases.  ACU’s staff will treat all consumers/members consistently and fairly and in compliance with fair lending laws.  ACU’s staff will be expected to offer assistance, encouragement and service in a fair, equitable and consistent manner.

**PURPOSE**

The purpose of this policy is to ensure ACU’s staff understands and fully complies with the provisions and protections provided to our consumer’s/member’s under the Fair Lending laws and regulations.  Fair Lending laws include the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (Regulation B), Home Mortgage Disclosure Act (Regulation C), the Fair Housing Act (FHA) and the NCUA’s Part 701.31 nondiscrimination requirements. 

**SCOPE**

The success of ACU’s Fair Lending Program is the responsibility of the entire organization.  ACU’s fair lending practices apply across all aspects of our operations, including marketing, loan origination, processing, underwriting, servicing, and collection activities.  ACU’s commitment to fair lending principles is demonstrated in terms of our structure and organization, employee accountability, monitoring and auditing programs, training and technology.  Our Chief Lending Officer is responsible for establishing practices designed to ensure that our operations reflect our strong commitment to fair lending and that all employees are aware of that commitment. 

**TYPES OF DISCRIMINATION**   

The courts have recognized three methods of proof of lending discrimination under the ECOA and the Fair Housing Act that credit union staff must be mindful of: 

**Overt evidence of  Discrimination** \- There is overt evidence of discrimination if the lender openly discriminates on a prohibited basis.  There also may be overt discrimination if a lender expresses, but does not act on, a discriminatory preference.

Overt discrimination may be one or more acts of discrimination by one or more loan officers, or it may be contained in a credit union policy that is overtly discriminatory.

**Comparative evidence of Disparate Treatment** \- 

Disparate treatment could occur if the credit union treated credit applicants differently based on one or more of the prohibited bases.  The difference in treatment does not have to be motivated by prejudice or a conscious intention to discriminate to be considered discriminatory.

Disparate treatment may involve unequal assistance being given to different applicants in the application process, or the presence or absence of discussion and investigation to clear up credit report problems, if this assistance to applicants varies on a prohibited basis.

**Disparate Impact –** 

If the credit union applies a policy or practice equally to credit applicants, but the policy or practice has a disproportionate adverse impact on applicants from a group protected against discrimination, the policy or practice is described as having a disparate impact.  Not every member of the group must be adversely affected for the practice to have a disparate impact.

A quantitative or statistical analysis is usually required to prove the existence of disparate impact.  If the credit union policy is based on “business necessity,” the credit union must be able to justify that a less discriminatory alternative policy does not exist.

**GATHERING, PROCESSING AND EVALUATING THE APPLICATION**

All prospective borrowers shall be encouraged to complete an application.  The Credit Union is required to obtain a written application for requests for a loan to purchase or refinance the purchase of a dwelling that is, or shall be, owner-occupied.  However, it is the policy of this credit union to require a written application to support all loan requests.  

No unnecessary information shall be gathered for the credit decision and credit union staff shall not ask prohibited questions. All credit decisions shall be made based on the creditworthiness of the applicant(s).  The credit union’s application forms shall state that income from alimony, child support, or separate maintenance payments need only be provided if the applicant is relying on that income for repayment of the credit requested.  

The age of the applicant may only be considered in determining the applicant’s ability to enter into a binding contract.  Credit may not be denied or terminated based on the applicant’s inability to obtain credit insurance due to their age. 

The credit union shall not require the signature of a spouse or person other than the credit applicant when the individual applicant qualifies for the credit requested.  When there is a joint applicant, the application shall include proper evidence that the applicants intended to apply for joint credit.

The credit union may request information about a spouse or former spouse of an applicant if: 

* the spouse shall be permitted to use the account;  
* the spouse shall be contractually liable on the account;  
* the applicant is relying on the spouse’s income, or the alimony, child support, or separate maintenance payments from a spouse or former spouse as a basis for repayment; or  
* the applicant is relying on property located in a community property state as a basis for repayment.

If an applicant applies for individual unsecured credit, credit union staff is prohibited from inquiring about the applicant's marital status, unless the applicant resides in a community property state or is relying on property located in such a state as a basis for repayment of the credit requested. 

If an application is for other than individual unsecured credit (joint and/or secured credit), credit union staff may inquire about the applicant's marital status, but may use only the terms married, unmarried, and separated. Credit union staff may explain that the category "unmarried" includes single, divorced, and widowed persons.

Credit union staff may not inquire about the sex of an applicant. An applicant may be requested to designate a title on an application form (such as Ms., Miss, Mr., or Mrs.) if the form discloses that the designation of a title is optional. An application form may otherwise use only terms that are neutral as to sex.

Credit union staff may not inquire about birth control practices, intentions concerning the bearing or rearing of children, or capability to bear children. Credit union staff may however inquire about the number and ages of an applicant's dependents or about dependent-related financial obligations or expenditures provided such information is requested without regard to sex, marital status, or any other prohibited basis.

Credit union staff may not inquire about the race, color, religion, or national origin of an applicant or any other person in connection with a credit transaction. However, credit union staff may inquire about an applicant's permanent residence and immigration status.

The Credit Union may require a qualified cosigner if the applicant does not qualify.  A signature may also be required on a security agreement for any joint owners of collateral that secures the loan.

**GOVERNMENT MONITORING INFORMATION (GMI)**

The credit union will gather certain information for government monitoring purposes in connection with credit applications primarily for the purchase or refinancing of a dwelling to be used as the applicant's principal residence where the extension of credit will be secured by the dwelling.  For purposes of GMI, "dwelling" means a residential structure that contains one to four units, whether or not that structure is attached to real property. The term includes, but is not limited to, an individual condominium or cooperative unit, and a mobile or other manufactured home. 

Credit union staff will request the following information as part of the application regarding the applicant(s): 

* ethnicity, using the categories Hispanic or Latino, and Not Hispanic or Latino; and race, using the categories American Indian or Alaskan Native, Asian, Black or African American; Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander, and White;   
* sex;   
* marital status, using the categories married, unmarried, and separated; and   
* age. 

The credit union staff will also inform the applicant(s) that the information regarding race or national origin, sex, marital status, and age is being requested by the Federal government for the purpose of monitoring compliance with Federal statutes that prohibits the credit union from discriminating against applicants on those bases.

**REAL ESTATE APPRAISALS**  

The credit union shall make available, to any requesting member/applicant, a copy of the appraisal used in connection with that member's real estate-related loan application in compliance with NCUA’s nondiscrimination regulation. The appraisal will be available for 25 months after the applicant has received a notice of action taken by the credit union on the real estate-related loan application.

Effective January 18, 2014 the credit union will be subject to Regulation B, Section 1002.14 for rules on providing appraisals and other evaluations that cover all first liens on dwellings, including closed-end mortgage loans and open-end loans as follows:

*    the credit union will provide a disclosure to applicants that they have the right to receive copies of appraisals and written valuations, and  
*    the credit union will automatically send a free copy of home appraisals and other written valuations promptly after they are completed, regardless of whether credit is extended, denied, incomplete, or withdrawn. This applies to all written valuations (not just appraisals) that are developed in connection with an application for a first lien on dwellings.

**NOTIFICATION REQUIREMENTS**  

The credit union will notify applicants of each loan decision reached by the credit union for any application received, and will strictly comply with the notification and adverse action notices required by the Equal Credit Opportunity Act and Regulation B.  The credit union will also comply with Section 615(a) of the Fair Credit Reporting Act when adverse action is taken against an applicant based in whole or in part on information in a consumer report.  In addition, if the credit score is used in taking adverse action the credit union will also include the required information relating to that score on the adverse action notice.       

The credit union will notify an applicant of action taken within:

* 30 days after receiving a completed application concerning the creditor's approval of, counteroffer to, or adverse action on the application;  
* 30 days after taking adverse action on an incomplete application,   
* 30 days after taking adverse action on an existing account; or  
* 90 days after notifying the applicant of a counteroffer if the applicant does not expressly accept or use the credit offered; or the credit union may choose to send an adverse action letter at the same time of the counteroffer within 30 days of receiving a completed application.

For incomplete applications, the credit union may send either a notice of incompleteness or an adverse-action notice. If a notice of incompleteness is sent, it will be specific about what information is missing and provide a reasonable time for the applicant to provide it. The notice will state that if the missing information is not provided within a designated time, no further consideration will be given to the application.

**RECORD RETENTION REQUIREMENTS**  

The credit union will comply with all Regulation B retention requirements and retain the original or a copy of the following documents for 25 months after the notice of action is taken:

* the application and the information obtained for purposes of monitoring compliance with anti-discrimination statutes;  
* any written information used in evaluating the request; and  
* a copy of the notification provided to an applicant and a statement of specific reasons for any adverse action.

All records related to prescreened solicitations will also be maintained for 25 months.  

**FAIR HOUSING ACT**

The Fair Housing Act provides for fair housing throughout the United States.  Credit union staff will not deny a loan or other financial assistance for the purpose of purchasing, constructing, improving, repairing, or maintaining a dwelling, nor may it discourage an application for such a loan, on the basis of the race, color, religion, handicap, familial status (having children under the age of 18), national origin, or sex of: 

* The loan applicant or joint applicant   
* Any person associated with the loan applicant or joint applicant   
* The present or prospective owners, lessees, tenants, or occupants of other dwellings in the vicinity of the dwelling 

Credit Union staff will ensure all credit-related decisions will be based on neutral credit factors such as the applicant’s income, credit history, length and stability of employment, ratio of income to current and proposed financial obligations, and ratio of the proposed principal balance of the loan to the value of the proposed property security. 

Unless a valid and well-documented exception exists, the credit union will not consider any of the following factors in connection with a real estate-related loan:

* the age or location of the dwelling;  
* the age or location of dwellings in the neighborhood of the dwelling;  
* the income level of residents in the neighborhood of the dwelling;  
* the zip code of the applicant’s current residence; or  
* previous home ownership.

In addition, credit union staff will not use the services of an appraiser who is known to improperly consider any of the prohibited bases.

**ADVERTISING AND LOBBY DISCLOSURES**

The credit union shall not engage in advertising in a manner that would discourage, on a prohibited basis, the making or pursuing of an application for credit.  As applicable, advertising shall include the Equal Housing Lender logotype or the term “Equal Housing Lender”.  The disclosure will be made in a manner appropriate to the advertising medium and format used that ensures it is clear and conspicuous.    
   
Each of the Credit Union’s locations shall maintain an Equal Housing Lender poster in the public lobby of the credit union and in the public area of each office where real estate related loans are made.  The notice must be clearly visible to the general public.

**HOME MORTGAGE DISCLOSURE ACT (HMDA)** 

The credit union will comply with the Home Mortgage Disclosure Act and properly compile and disclose data about home purchase loans, home improvement loans, and refinancings that we originate or purchase, or for which we receive applications.  Reference ACU’s HMDA Policy.

**THIRD PARTIES**

Applicable agreements and contracts with third party service providers will contain language which indicates the third party acknowledges and agrees to abide by fair lending laws. 

**PERIODIC REVIEWS**

Third party services will be contracted to conduct periodic risk based reviews in all areas of fair lending.  The reviewers will utilize NCUA’s Fair Lending Guide to establish the review areas and to make use of the various checklist tools available.  The Executive Management and the Board of Directors will be provided with a written report for all periodic fair lending reviews conducted.  

**TRAINING**

The Training Solutions Manager will provide and/or facilitate the instruction of periodic fair lending training to all applicable staff based on job responsibilities. The ongoing training program will provide staff with the basic principles and core requirements of the various fair lending laws and regulations that impact their job functions. 	

**REFERENCES**

# Regulation B, Equal Credit Opportunity Act, 12 CFR 1002

# Regulation C, Home Mortgage Disclosure Act, 12 CFR 1003

# Fair Credit Reporting Act, 12 CFR 1022 as amended by The Fair and Accurate Credit Transaction Act

# Fair Housing Act 42 USC 3601

**QUALITY RECORDS**

| Title | Location Kept | Duration Kept | Disposal Method |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Fair Lending Policy | Policy Tech | Indefinite | Archived |
"""
- AUTHORITY_HINTS: - **ECOA / Reg B (12 CFR Part 1002)** — covers all credit, including inquiries (§1002.5), evaluation (§1002.6), action-taken notices (§1002.9), retention (§1002.12), monitoring (§1002.13), appraisals/ROV (§1002.14), definitions (§1002.2), and the prohibition (§1002.4).
- **FHA / HUD / NCUA** — 42 USC §3605; 24 CFR Part 100; 12 CFR 701.31 — govern real-estate-related transactions and signage/advertising.
- **HMDA / Reg C (12 CFR Part 1003, incl. §1003.4)** — governs GMI collection, the LAR, and submission/disclosure where applicable.
- **TILA / Reg Z (12 CFR §1026.24; §1026.36(d),(e))** — govern advertising, Loan Originator compensation, and anti-steering.
- **ADA (28 CFR Part 36; 29 CFR Part 1630)** — access and reasonable accommodation.
- **FCRA / Reg V (15 USC §1681 et seq.; §615), interagency appraisal-independence rules, and CFPB UDAAP standards** — related requirements affecting adverse-action content and fair treatment.
- DESIGN_NOTES: """# System Design Notes — Engineering Vocabulary

> Generated from `vocabulary.json` — **Cassandra Banking Core API** v1.0.0
> Parsed at 2026-03-13T21:51:37.890Z
> Totals: 14 entities · 252 fields · 0 computed fields · 0 events · 85 endpoints · 0 state machines · 0 plugins

This block is a verbatim dump of the current engineering vocabulary. Downstream regeneration decides what to reference in the Design Overlay v2 control blocks — do not pre-filter.

## Entities and fields

### `account`  (schema: `Account` · 15 fields · retention: 7y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ✓ |  |  | Unique account identifier. |
| `entity_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | Primary owning entity. Immutable after creation. This is the entity under which the account appears for 5300 reporting and BSA monitoring. |
| `account_type` | string (enum: checking, savings, money_market) | ✓ |  |  | Ledger account type. Maps to 5300 account codes via the bookkeeping layer. - `checking` — Share draft account (5300 code 025B) - `savings`… |
| `status` | string (enum: open, frozen, closed) | ✓ |  |  | Account lifecycle state. - `open` — Active and operational. - `frozen` — Temporarily suspended (no debits; credits may still post). Reversi… |
| `lock_type` | string (enum: none, compliance, fraud, legal, admin) | ✓ |  |  | Orthogonal lock applied independently of entity status. When lock_type is not `none`, the entity is restricted from certain operations even… |
| `dormancy_status` | string (enum: active, dormant, abandoned) | ✓ |  |  | Orthogonal dormancy classification, tracked independently of account status. Driven by state-specific inactivity periods (typically 12 mont… |
| `currency` | string | ✓ |  |  | ISO 4217 currency code. Only USD supported. |
| `balances` | object | ✓ |  |  | Balance fields sourced from the Blnk Deploy ledger. These are read-only computed values — all mutations happen through transaction endpoint… |
| `balances.balance` | integer |  |  |  | Current available balance (cents). Credits minus debits, excluding inflight. |
| `balances.credit_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Lifetime credit total (cents). |
| `balances.debit_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Lifetime debit total (cents). |
| `balances.inflight_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Net pending transaction amount (cents). Represents authorized but unsettled transactions (e.g., card authorizations). Managed by Blnk's QUE… |
| `balances.inflight_credit_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Pending inbound credits (cents). |
| `balances.inflight_debit_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Pending outbound debits (cents). |
| `bookkeeping` | object | ✓ |  |  | Shadow bookkeeping layer fields for 5300 regulatory reporting and FBO segregation. These are managed by Cassandra, not sourced from Blnk. |
| `bookkeeping.account_code_5300` | string |  |  |  | NCUA 5300 account code (e.g., 025B for share drafts, 010 for regular shares). Assigned automatically based on account type; overridable by… |
| `bookkeeping.schedule_a_code` | string |  |  |  | 5300 Schedule A line item code for balance sheet classification. Used by the 5300 Reporter consumer for aggregated reporting. |
| `bookkeeping.fbo_classification` | string (enum: customer_balance, commingled_cash, settlement) |  |  |  | FBO sub-account classification for the bookkeeping layer. - `customer_balance` — Holds attributed to a specific end-user entity. - `comming… |
| `bookkeeping.locked_amount` | integer |  |  |  | Amount held by BSA/compliance controls (cents). Separate from Blnk inflight holds — this represents funds frozen by the control engine (e.g… |
| `owners` | array<object> | ✓ |  |  | All entities with ownership or authority over this account. At minimum, includes the primary owner matching entity_id. Joint ownership is m… |
| `blnk_ledger_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding ledger ID in Blnk Deploy. Opaque reference for reconciliation. |
| `account_numbers` | array<object> |  |  |  | Summary of account numbers associated with this account. Use the Account Numbers endpoints for full details and management. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  | Arbitrary key-value pairs for partner use. |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `ach_transfer`  (schema: `AchTransfer` · 23 fields · retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| `account_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | The ledger account being debited or credited. |
| `account_number_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | The specific account number used for this transfer. Determines the routing/account number pair that appears in the ACH file. |
| `direction` | string (enum: credit, debit) | ✓ |  |  | Direction of the ACH transfer from the perspective of this account. - `credit` — Push money out (originate a payment to an external party).… |
| `amount` | integer | ✓ |  |  | Transfer amount in cents. Must be positive. |
| `currency` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending_approval, submitted, settled, returned, rejected, canceled) | ✓ |  |  | ACH transfer lifecycle state. - `pending_approval` — Created, awaiting control engine evaluation. - `submitted` — Controls passed, ACH file… |
| `window` | string (enum: standard, same_day) | ✓ |  |  | ACH processing window. - `standard` — Next-day settlement (standard ACH). - `same_day` — Same-day ACH (additional fee may apply, cutoff tim… |

| `counterparty.account_number` | string | ✓ | ✓ |  | Account number at the receiving institution. |
| `counterparty.account_type` | string (enum: checking, savings) | ✓ |  |  | Account type at the receiving institution. |
| `counterparty.name` | string | ✓ | ✓ |  | Receiver name as it will appear in the ACH file. Max 22 characters per NACHA format. |
| `description` | string |  |  |  | Company entry description in the ACH batch header. Max 10 characters per NACHA format (e.g., "PAYROLL", "PAYMENT"). |
| `company_name` | string |  |  |  | Originator company name in the ACH batch header. Max 16 characters per NACHA format. Defaults to partner's registered name. |
| `effective_date` | string |  |  |  | The date the transfer will settle at the Fed. Set by the system based on the processing window and submission time. For same-day ACH, this… |
| `expected_settlement` | string |  |  |  | Estimated settlement timestamp. More precise than effective_date — accounts for Fed processing windows, weekends, and holidays. |
| `settled_at` | string |  |  |  | Actual settlement timestamp. Populated when status becomes `settled`. |
| `returned_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp of the return. Populated when status becomes `returned`. |
| `return_code` | string |  |  |  | NACHA return reason code (e.g., R01 Insufficient Funds, R02 Account Closed, R03 No Account, R04 Invalid Account Number). Only present when… |
| `return_reason` | string |  |  |  | Human-readable return reason. Only present when status is `returned`. |
| `rejection_reason` | string |  |  |  | Explanation of why the control engine rejected this transfer. Only present when status is `rejected`. |
| `control_results` | array<object> |  |  |  | Results of all control engine evaluations for this transfer. Populated at creation time (when the control engine gates the transfer). |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding transaction ID in Blnk Deploy. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `wire_transfer`  (schema: `WireTransfer` · 21 fields · retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| `amount` | integer | ✓ |  |  | Transfer amount in cents. |
| `currency` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending_approval, submitted, completed, return_requested, returned, rejected, …(+1)) | ✓ |  |  | Wire transfer lifecycle state. - `pending_approval` — Created, awaiting control engine evaluation. - `submitted` — Controls passed, Fedwire… |
| `beneficiary` | object |  |  |  | The receiving party and their bank details for an outbound wire. |
| `beneficiary.name` | string | ✓ | ✓ |  | Beneficiary name as it will appear on the Fedwire message. |
| `beneficiary.routing_number` | string | ✓ |  |  | ABA routing number of the beneficiary's bank. |
| `beneficiary.account_number` | string | ✓ | ✓ |  | Account number at the beneficiary's bank. |
| `beneficiary.bank_name` | string |  |  |  | Name of the beneficiary's bank. Optional — if omitted, resolved automatically from the routing number via the Fed directory. |
| `beneficiary.address` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `beneficiary.address.line1` | string | ✓ | ✓ |  | Street address line 1. |
| `beneficiary.address.line2` | string |  |  |  | Street address line 2 (apt, suite, unit, etc.). |
| `beneficiary.address.city` | string | ✓ | ✓ |  |  |
| `beneficiary.address.state` | string | ✓ |  |  | Two-letter US state or territory code (ISO 3166-2:US). |
| `beneficiary.address.postal_code` | string | ✓ | ✓ |  | US ZIP code (5-digit or ZIP+4). |
| `beneficiary.address.country` | string | ✓ |  |  | ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. |
| `originator` | object |  |  |  | Originator information from an inbound Fedwire message. Read-only — populated by the aggregator when routing an inbound wire. |
| `originator.name` | string |  | ✓ |  |  |
| `originator.routing_number` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.account_number` | string |  | ✓ |  |  |

| `purpose` | string |  |  |  | Originator-to-beneficiary information (OBI). Free-text description of the wire purpose, included in the Fedwire message. |
| `imad` | string |  |  |  | Input Message Accountability Data — the Fed's unique identifier for this Fedwire message. Populated after submission. Used for tracing and… |
| `omad` | string |  |  |  | Output Message Accountability Data — assigned by the receiving Fed bank. Populated after the wire is acknowledged. |
| `submitted_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp when the Fedwire message was submitted. |
| `completed_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp when the wire completed. Wires are typically same-day. |
| `return_request` | object |  |  |  | Details of the return request, if one has been filed. Only present when status is `return_requested` or `returned`. |
| `rejection_reason` | string |  |  |  | Explanation of why the control engine rejected this wire. Only present when status is `rejected`. |
| `control_results` | array<object> |  |  |  | Results of control engine evaluations. |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding transaction ID in Blnk Deploy. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `verification`  (schema: `Verification` · 14 fields · retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ✓ |  |  | Unique verification identifier. |
| `entity_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | The entity being verified. |
| `type` | string (enum: kyc, kyb) | ✓ |  |  | Whether this is identity verification for a person (KYC) or a business (KYB). Trusts use KYB. Joint entities are verified through their ind… |
| `provider` | string (enum: alloy, persona, socure, middesk, partner_provided) | ✓ |  |  | The identity verification provider used for this check. - `alloy`, `persona`, `socure`, `middesk` — Cassandra-managed providers. The system… |
| `trust_level` | string (enum: full, verify_watchlist_only) |  |  |  | Trust level for partner-provided verifications (from partner configuration). - `full` — Cassandra trusts the partner's identity verificatio… |
| `status` | string (enum: pending, in_review, approved, denied, expired) | ✓ |  |  | Status of this individual verification attempt. - `pending` — Verification submitted to provider, awaiting result. - `in_review` — Provider… |
| `ofac_result` | object | ✓ |  |  | Result of the mandatory OFAC screening. OFAC screening is a compliance floor control (BA-05) and runs on every verification regardless of p… |
| `ofac_result.screened_at` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `ofac_result.match_status` | string (enum: clear, potential_match, confirmed_match) |  |  |  | - `clear` — No matches found on SDN, consolidated sanctions, or PEP lists. - `potential_match` — Possible match requiring compliance office… |
| `ofac_result.matched_lists` | array<string> |  |  |  | Names of lists that returned matches (e.g., "SDN", "Consolidated Sanctions", "PEP", "314a"). Empty array when match_status is `clear`. |
| `ofac_result.match_score` | number |  |  |  | Confidence score of the strongest match (0-100). Only meaningful when match_status is `potential_match`. Used by compliance officers to pri… |
| `provider_result` | object |  |  |  | Normalized verification result from the provider. Cassandra translates each provider's native response into this common structure. |
| `provider_result.provider_reference` | string |  |  |  | The provider's native reference ID for this verification (e.g., Alloy evaluation_token, Persona inquiry_id). |
| `provider_result.provider_status` | string |  |  |  | The provider's native status string, preserved for debugging. Cassandra normalizes this into the verification's top-level `status`. |
| `provider_result.identity_verified` | boolean |  |  |  | Whether the provider confirmed the identity matches the provided data. |
| `provider_result.document_verified` | boolean |  |  |  | Whether identity documents were verified (if required by risk tier). Null if document verification was not required. |
| `provider_result.risk_score` | number |  |  |  | Provider's risk score, normalized to 0-100 range. Higher = riskier. Used by CDD controls for risk tiering. |
| `provider_result.risk_signals` | array<string> |  |  |  | Risk signal tags from the provider (e.g., "address_mismatch", "ssn_issued_prior_to_dob", "high_risk_jurisdiction"). Used by the control eng… |
| `provider_result.raw_response` | object |  |  |  | The complete provider response, stored verbatim for audit purposes. Only returned when the requesting token has compliance-scoped permissio… |
| `denial_reasons` | array<string> |  |  |  | Machine-readable reasons for denial (e.g., "identity_not_verified", "document_mismatch", "high_risk_jurisdiction", "ofac_match"). Only pres… |
| `expires_at` | string |  |  |  | When this verification result expires per CDD refresh policy. After expiry, the entity's kyc_status transitions to `expired` and a new veri… |
| `triggered_by` | string (enum: initial, cdd_refresh, information_change, manual) |  |  |  | What triggered this verification. - `initial` — First-time verification on entity creation. - `cdd_refresh` — Periodic re-verification per… |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `card`  (schema: `Card` · 16 fields · retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ✓ |  |  | Unique card identifier. |
| `account_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | The ledger account this card draws from / credits to. |
| `entity_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | The entity this card is issued to. Must be a person entity. Immutable after creation. |
| `card_type` | string (enum: virtual, physical) | ✓ |  |  | - `virtual` — Instantly issued, PAN available immediately via secure details endpoint. - `physical` — Requires fulfillment (mailing). PAN a… |
| `form_factor` | string (enum: debit, prepaid) | ✓ |  |  | Card form factor for network classification. - `debit` — Linked to a deposit account with overdraft rules. - `prepaid` — Balance-limited, n… |
| `network` | string (enum: visa, mastercard) |  |  |  | Card network, determined by the BIN allocated to this instance. |
| `status` | string (enum: inactive, active, frozen, closed) | ✓ |  |  | Card lifecycle state, driven by the adapter interface (createCard, activateCard, freezeCard, closeCard). - `inactive` — Card created but no… |
| `last_four` | string | ✓ |  |  | Last four digits of the card number (PAN). |
| `expiration_month` | integer | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `expiration_year` | integer | ✓ |  |  | Four-digit expiration year. |
| `shipping` | object |  |  |  | Shipping details for physical cards. Only present when `card_type` is `physical`. Null for virtual cards. |
| `shipping.status` | string (enum: pending, shipped, delivered, returned) |  |  |  |  |
| `shipping.tracking_number` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `shipping.carrier` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `shipping.shipped_at` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `shipping.estimated_delivery` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `spend_controls` | object |  |  |  | Optional per-card spending limits. These are enforced in addition to account-level controls and the control engine's velocity limits. |
| `spend_controls.daily_limit` | integer |  |  |  | Maximum daily spend in cents. Null means no per-card daily limit. |
| `spend_controls.monthly_limit` | integer |  |  |  | Maximum monthly spend in cents. |
| `spend_controls.per_transaction_limit` | integer |  |  |  | Maximum single transaction amount in cents. |
| `spend_controls.allowed_mcc_codes` | array<string> |  |  |  | Whitelist of allowed Merchant Category Codes. If populated, only transactions at merchants with these MCCs are approved. Empty array means… |
| `spend_controls.blocked_mcc_codes` | array<string> |  |  |  | Blacklist of blocked MCCs. Transactions at merchants with these MCCs are declined. Evaluated after allowed_mcc_codes. |
| `processor_reference` | string |  |  |  | Card reference ID at the processor (Marqeta card_token or Lithic card_id). Opaque — used internally for adapter operations. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `card_authorization`  (schema: `CardAuthorization` · 14 fields · retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `card_id` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `account_id` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending, approved, declined, reversed, expired) | ✓ |  |  | Authorization lifecycle. - `pending` — Authorization received, control engine evaluating (transient, sub-2-second). - `approved` — Authoriz… |
| `amount` | integer | ✓ |  |  | Authorization amount in cents. |
| `currency` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `merchant` | object | ✓ |  |  | Merchant details from the authorization request. |
| `merchant.name` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `merchant.mcc` | string |  |  |  | Merchant Category Code. |
| `merchant.city` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `merchant.state` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `merchant.country` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `merchant.acceptor_id` | string |  |  |  | Card acceptor ID from the processor. |
| `decline_reason` | string (enum: insufficient_funds, card_frozen, card_closed, account_frozen, account_locked, spend_limit_exceeded, …(+5)) |  |  |  | Machine-readable reason for declining an authorization. |
| `control_results` | array<object> |  |  |  | Control engine results for this authorization. Includes balance check, velocity, OFAC, fraud, and spend control evaluations. |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Blnk inflight transaction ID (for approved authorizations). Null for declined authorizations. |
| `processor_reference` | string |  |  |  | Processor's native authorization reference. |
| `settled_at` | string |  |  |  | Settlement timestamp. Populated when the inflight transaction commits in Blnk (processor sends settlement webhook). Null until settled. |
| `settlement_amount` | integer |  |  |  | Final settlement amount in cents. May differ from the authorization amount (e.g., tip added, partial fulfillment). |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `bookkeeping_entry`  (schema: `BookkeepingEntry` · 17 fields · retention: 7y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ✓ |  |  | Unique bookkeeping entry identifier. |
| `account_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | The ledger account this entry pertains to. |
| `account_number_id` | string |  |  |  | The specific account number involved (if applicable). Null for account-level entries (e.g., interest, fees). |
| `entity_id` | string |  |  |  | The entity attributed for FBO segregation. Derived from the account's primary owner or the account number's informational_entity_id, depend… |
| `entry_type` | string (enum: debit, credit, hold_placed, hold_released, hold_seized) | ✓ |  |  | Type of bookkeeping entry. - `debit` — Funds left the account (matches a Blnk debit transaction). - `credit` — Funds entered the account (m… |
| `source` | string (enum: ach_transfer, wire_transfer, book_transfer, card_authorization, card_settlement, fee, …(+3)) | ✓ |  |  | The originating transaction type that produced this bookkeeping entry. Maps 1:1 to the domain event that triggered the Blnk webhook. |
| `source_id` | string |  |  |  | ID of the originating resource (e.g., ach_5kRb3m for an ACH transfer, auth_2nRb7m for a card authorization). Allows joining bookkeeping ent… |
| `amount` | integer | ✓ |  |  | Entry amount in cents. Always positive — direction is conveyed by entry_type. |
| `currency` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `running_balance` | integer |  |  |  | Account balance after this entry was applied (cents). Provides a verifiable audit trail — each entry's running_balance should equal the pre… |
| `account_code_5300` | string | ✓ |  |  | NCUA 5300 account code for this entry (e.g., 025B for share drafts, 010 for regular shares). Inherited from the account's bookkeeping tags… |
| `schedule_a_code` | string |  |  |  | 5300 Schedule A line item code for balance sheet classification. Used by the 5300 Reporter for aggregated reporting. |
| `fbo_classification` | string (enum: customer_balance, commingled_cash, settlement) | ✓ |  |  | FBO sub-account classification for the bookkeeping layer. - `customer_balance` — Holds attributed to a specific end-user entity. - `comming… |
| `blnk_transaction_id` | string |  |  |  | Corresponding Blnk Deploy transaction ID. Null for compliance hold entries (which are Cassandra-native, not Blnk transactions). |
| `description` | string |  |  |  | System-generated description of the entry (e.g., "ACH credit from John Doe via 271070801", "Compliance hold placed — BA-05 OFAC match"). |
| `effective_date` | string |  |  |  | The business date this entry is effective for regulatory reporting. May differ from created_at for entries posted after business hours or f… |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `event`  (schema: `Event` · 11 fields · retention: 7y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ✓ |  |  | Unique event identifier. |
| `type` | string (enum: entity.created, entity.activated, entity.disabled, entity.archived, entity.updated, entity.locked, …(+51)) | ✓ |  |  | Domain event type. Events are emitted on every state transition and significant operation. Partners receive these via webhook delivery. |
| `resource_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | ID of the resource that changed (e.g., ent_abc123, acct_xyz789). |
| `resource_type` | string | ✓ |  |  | Type of the resource (e.g., entity, account, ach_transfer). |
| `data` | object |  |  |  | Snapshot of the resource at the time of the event. Contains the full resource representation (same schema as the corresponding GET endpoint… |
| `previous_data` | object |  |  |  | For update events, the resource state before the change. Only includes fields that changed. Null for creation events. |
| `delivery_status` | string (enum: pending, delivered, failed, expired) |  |  |  | Delivery status for a specific webhook delivery attempt. - `pending` — Queued for delivery (or retrying). - `delivered` — Partner endpoint… |
| `delivery_attempts` | integer |  |  |  | Number of webhook delivery attempts made so far (max 12). |
| `last_delivery_attempt_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp of the most recent delivery attempt. |
| `delivered_at` | string |  |  |  | Timestamp of successful delivery. Null if not yet delivered. |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `webhook_endpoint`  (schema: `WebhookEndpoint` · 9 fields)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ✓ |  |  | Unique webhook endpoint identifier. |
| `url` | string | ✓ |  |  | The HTTPS URL that receives webhook POST requests. Must use HTTPS (HTTP rejected). Must return 2xx within 30 seconds to be considered a suc… |
| `status` | string (enum: active, disabled) | ✓ |  |  | - `active` — Events are delivered to this endpoint. - `disabled` — Delivery is paused. Events accumulate and can be retrieved via the backf… |
| `subscribed_events` | array<string> | ✓ |  |  | List of event type patterns this endpoint receives. Supports exact types (e.g., "ach_transfer.settled") and wildcard prefixes (e.g., "ach_t… |
| `signing_secret` | string |  |  |  | HMAC-SHA256 signing secret for verifying webhook payloads. Only returned once at creation time. Use to verify the `X-Cassandra-Signature` h… |
| `delivery_stats` | object |  |  |  | Rolling delivery statistics for this endpoint. |
| `delivery_stats.total_delivered` | integer |  |  |  |  |
| `delivery_stats.total_failed` | integer |  |  |  |  |
| `delivery_stats.last_delivered_at` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `delivery_stats.last_failed_at` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `delivery_stats.current_failure_streak` | integer |  |  |  | Consecutive failed deliveries. When this reaches a threshold, the endpoint may be automatically disabled and the partner alerted. |
| `metadata` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `updated_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `fbo_position`  (schema: `FboPosition` · 6 fields)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `instance_id` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `balance` | integer | ✓ |  |  | Current FBO balance for this instance (cents). Sum of all settled inbound minus all settled outbound for this instance's accounts. |
| `held_amount` | integer | ✓ |  |  | Amount currently reserved for pending outbound payments (cents). Held via the reserve saga pattern (Decision 28, step 4). |
| `available_balance` | integer | ✓ |  |  | Effective available balance (cents). Computed as `balance - held_amount`. This is the amount the Origination API checks against when approv… |
| `last_event_sequence` | integer | ✓ |  |  | The sequence_id of the most recent event processed by the Payment Hub consumer for this instance. Used for staleness detection. |
| `updated_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

### `inbound_payment`  (schema: `InboundPayment` · 9 fields · retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `id` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `payment_type` | string (enum: ach_credit, ach_debit, wire, rtp) | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `account_number` | string | ✓ |  |  | The 12-digit account number this payment was routed to. |
| `amount` | integer | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `currency` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `status` | string (enum: pending, delivered, acknowledged) | ✓ |  |  | - `pending` — Aggregator has the payment, not yet pushed to instance. - `delivered` — Pushed to instance via webhook. - `acknowledged` — In… |
| `originator` | object |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.name` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.routing_number` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `originator.account_number` | string |  |  |  |  |
| `reference` | string |  |  |  | Originator reference or trace number. |
| `received_at` | string | ✓ |  |  | When the aggregator received this payment from the Fed. |

### `bsa_alert`  (schema: `BsaAlert` · 9 fields · retention: 5y)

| Field | Type | Required | PII | Bound controls | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `alert_id` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |
| `event_id` | string | ✓ |  |  | The event that triggered this alert. |
| `alert_type` | string (enum: ctr_threshold, structuring_suspected, cross_fintech_activity, sar_candidate, velocity_anomaly, watchlist_hit) | ✓ |  |  | Type of BSA alert generated by the BSA Approver (real-time) or BSA Reporter (scheduled). |
| `entity_hash` | string | ✓ |  |  | Hashed entity identifier (SSN/EIN hash). Used for per-entity lookups without exposing PII in the alert table. |
| `instance_id` | string |  |  |  | The fintech instance where the triggering event originated. For cross-fintech alerts, this is the instance of the most recent triggering ev… |
| `status` | string (enum: open, under_review, escalated, cleared, block) | ✓ |  |  | BSA alert lifecycle. - `open` — New alert, not yet reviewed. - `under_review` — Analyst is investigating. - `escalated` — Elevated to SAR c… |
| `requires_lookback` | boolean |  |  |  | Whether the BSA Reporter should run a deep historical analysis on this alert. Set by the BSA Approver when it detects patterns that need 90… |
| `details` | object |  |  |  | Alert-specific details. Structure varies by alert_type. Examples: transaction amounts, pattern description, matched entity information for… |
| `created_at` | string | ✓ |  |  |  |

## Events

> ⚠️  **Vocabulary warning — no events defined.** The `events` array in vocabulary.json is empty. Any `(event.code)` reference the policy wants to use must be introduced by engineering and registered in the spec before the Design Overlay v2 blocks can cite it. The endpoints list below is provided as candidate signals from which engineering could derive event codes (e.g., `POST /accounts` → `account.created`).

## Endpoints

| Method | Path | Summary | Control refs | Audit events |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `GET` | `/entities` | List entities |  |  |
| `GET` | `/entities/{entity_id}` | Retrieve an entity |  |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/person` | Create a person entity | CD-01, CD-04, BA-03, BA-05 |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/business` | Create a business entity | CD-01, CD-04, CD-05, BA-03, BA-05 |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/trust` | Create a trust entity | CD-01, CD-04, BA-03, BA-05 |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/joint` | Create a joint entity | BA-05 |  |
| `PATCH` | `/entities/person/{entity_id}` | Update a person entity | CD-09, BA-05 |  |
| `PATCH` | `/entities/business/{entity_id}` | Update a business entity | CD-05, CD-09 |  |
| `PATCH` | `/entities/trust/{entity_id}` | Update a trust entity | CD-09 |  |
| `PATCH` | `/entities/joint/{entity_id}` | Update a joint entity |  |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/{entity_id}/status` | Transition entity status | BA-05, CD-08 |  |
| `PUT` | `/entities/{entity_id}/lock` | Update entity lock |  |  |
| `GET` | `/accounts` | List accounts |  |  |
| `POST` | `/accounts` | Create an account | CD-04, CD-03, CD-12, BA-05 |  |
| `GET` | `/accounts/{account_id}` | Retrieve an account |  |  |
| `PATCH` | `/accounts/{account_id}` | Update an account | CD-05 |  |
| `POST` | `/accounts/{account_id}/status` | Transition account status | CD-12 |  |
| `PUT` | `/accounts/{account_id}/lock` | Update account lock |  |  |
| `GET` | `/accounts/{account_id}/account-numbers` | List account numbers for an account |  |  |
| `POST` | `/accounts/{account_id}/account-numbers` | Create an additional account number | BA-05 |  |
| `GET` | `/account-numbers/{account_number_id}` | Retrieve an account number |  |  |
| `PATCH` | `/account-numbers/{account_number_id}` | Update an account number |  |  |
| `POST` | `/account-numbers/{account_number_id}/status` | Transition account number status |  |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/ach` | List ACH transfers |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/ach` | Create an ACH transfer | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10, CD-06 |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/ach/{ach_transfer_id}` | Retrieve an ACH transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/ach/{ach_transfer_id}/cancel` | Cancel an ACH transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/ach/batch` | Create a batch of ACH transfers | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10, CD-06 |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/book` | List book transfers |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/book` | Create a book transfer | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08 |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/book/{book_transfer_id}` | Retrieve a book transfer |  |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/wire` | List wire transfers |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/wire` | Create a wire transfer | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10 |  |
| `GET` | `/transfers/wire/{wire_transfer_id}` | Retrieve a wire transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/wire/{wire_transfer_id}/cancel` | Cancel a wire transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/transfers/wire/{wire_transfer_id}/return-request` | Request a wire return |  |  |
| `GET` | `/entities/{entity_id}/verifications` | List verifications for an entity |  |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/{entity_id}/verifications` | Initiate a verification | CD-01, CD-04, BA-03, BA-05, BA-20 |  |
| `POST` | `/entities/{entity_id}/verifications/attest` | Submit a partner-provided KYC/KYB attestation | CD-01, CD-10, BA-05, BA-20 |  |
| `GET` | `/verifications/{verification_id}` | Retrieve a verification |  |  |
| `GET` | `/cards` | List cards |  |  |
| `POST` | `/cards` | Create a card | BA-05, CD-04 |  |
| `GET` | `/cards/{card_id}` | Retrieve a card |  |  |
| `PATCH` | `/cards/{card_id}` | Update a card |  |  |
| `POST` | `/cards/{card_id}/status` | Transition card status |  |  |
| `GET` | `/cards/{card_id}/sensitive` | Retrieve sensitive card details | PR-01 |  |
| `GET` | `/cards/{card_id}/authorizations` | List authorizations for a card |  |  |
| `GET` | `/authorizations/{authorization_id}` | Retrieve a card authorization |  |  |
| `GET` | `/accounts/{account_id}/bookkeeping-entries` | List bookkeeping entries for an account |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bookkeeping-entries/{entry_id}` | Retrieve a bookkeeping entry |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bookkeeping/summary` | Get aggregated bookkeeping summary |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bookkeeping/reconciliation` | Run reconciliation check |  |  |
| `GET` | `/events` | List events |  |  |
| `GET` | `/events/{event_id}` | Retrieve an event |  |  |
| `POST` | `/events/{event_id}/retry` | Retry event delivery |  |  |
| `GET` | `/webhooks` | List webhook endpoints |  |  |
| `POST` | `/webhooks` | Create a webhook endpoint |  |  |
| `GET` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}` | Retrieve a webhook endpoint |  |  |
| `PATCH` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}` | Update a webhook endpoint |  |  |
| `DELETE` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}` | Delete a webhook endpoint |  |  |
| `POST` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}/rotate-secret` | Rotate webhook signing secret |  |  |
| `POST` | `/webhooks/{webhook_endpoint_id}/test` | Send a test webhook |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/kyc/approve` | Simulate: Approve KYC verification |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/kyc/deny` | Simulate: Deny KYC verification |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/ach/receive` | Simulate: Receive an inbound ACH credit |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/ach/settle` | Simulate: Settle an outbound ACH transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/ach/return` | Simulate: Return a settled ACH transfer |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/wire/receive` | Simulate: Receive an inbound wire |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/wire/settle` | Simulate: Complete an outbound wire |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/card/authorize` | Simulate: Card authorization |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/card/settle` | Simulate: Card settlement |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/simulate/time/advance` | Simulate: Advance sandbox clock |  |  |
| `POST` | `/sandbox/reset` | Reset sandbox environment |  |  |
| `POST` | `/auth/token` | Obtain an aggregator access token |  |  |
| `GET` | `/fbo/{instance_id}/position` | Get FBO position for an instance |  |  |
| `GET` | `/fbo/{instance_id}/available-balance` | Get available balance for an instance |  |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/ach/originate` | Originate an ACH payment | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08 |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/wire/originate` | Originate a wire payment | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10 |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/wire/prepare` | Prepare a wire payment (phase 1 of 2) | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08, BA-10 |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/wire/confirm` | Confirm a prepared wire payment (phase 2 of 2) |  |  |
| `POST` | `/payments/rtp/originate` | Originate an RTP payment | BA-05, BA-06, BA-07, BA-08 |  |
| `GET` | `/inbound/{instance_id}` | List inbound payments for an instance |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bsa/alerts` | List BSA alerts |  |  |
| `GET` | `/bsa/alerts/{alert_id}` | Retrieve a BSA alert |  |  |
| `GET` | `/consumers/health` | Get aggregator consumer health |  |  |

## State machines

_No state machines defined in vocabulary.json._

## Plugins

_No plugins defined in vocabulary.json._
"""


LOCAL OVERRIDES (apply after all meta-prompt rules):
{{Usually empty. Put anything here that should override the shared
`meta-prompt.md` for THIS policy only — a different control-ID prefix,
extra required sections, a specific citation style, etc. The regeneration
task applies overrides AFTER the shared meta-prompt, so instructions here
win.}}
